entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a multi-level architecture that retains user, session, and agent states. It utilizes a token-efficient single-pass extraction algorithm and hybrid retrieval to fuse semantic, keyword, and entity-linked signals.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'agent brain' features a self-wiring knowledge graph with typed entity links for sophisticated relationship mapping. It supports hybrid vector and graph-based retrieval and connects via MCP, CLI, or OAuth 2.1 for secure remote operations.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'retain, recall, and reflect' workflow that allows agents to learn and improve from past interactions over time. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management and supports per-user isolation through custom metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that maintains evolving context for users, agents, and groups across Python and TypeScript SDKs. It enables natural language querying of interaction histories and formats context to be natively compatible with OpenAI-style APIs.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses structured traces and real evaluation to iteratively improve agent performance on complex tasks. It facilitates multi-role cooperation for strategy refinement and knowledge curation across various model providers.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A specialized plugin for Hermes Agent that uses a hierarchical DAG structure to ensure lossless context management and depth-aware compaction. It includes a SQLite-backed store and dedicated tools for grepping or expanding compacted history without losing raw message data.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library, enabling private cross-session persistence specifically for Hermes Agent users. It supports local inference servers and includes automated scripts for deploying a full stack with Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a hybrid retrieval system combining vector search, BM25, and causal graph traversal to share memory across Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenClaw.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on inference efficiency, ContextPilot optimizes long-context LLM performance through prefix reordering and redundant block deduplication. It natively integrates with Hermes Agent and vLLM to boost cache hits and reduce input token usage.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur utilizes an open YAML 'engram' format to create a shared memory layer for multi-agent systems with zero API costs. It automates memory injection for Hermes and coding agents using a hybrid of BM25 and local embedding search.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases, documents, and media with automatic OCR and summarization. It uses edge tags to build bidirectional relationships between notes, accessible to agents via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system built on SQLite that offers sub-millisecond latency for local-first Hermes Agent deployments. It features native vector and full-text search alongside automated episodic memory consolidation.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses SQLite-vec and GGUF models for local-first storage and provides an MCP-native server for seamless client configuration.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry-standard frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks in cloud security and forensics while remaining compatible with the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It is designed to unify skill libraries across multiple devices and sessions, offering native integration for Hermes and Codex.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a comprehensive word replacement table and a two-pass audit process to ensure Hermes produces more natural, human-like prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that offers modular installation of skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It provides Hermes with a standardized framework for professional services compatible with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of skill metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions using specialized presets for machine learning and architecture. It features automated self-checking loops and supports advanced elements like animated connectors and tensor shapes.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-Hermes empowers the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracles, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes a local A/B testing framework to help developers evaluate and refine how Hermes handles smart contract data.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill enables literate programming by weaving together prose, diagrams, and source code into synchronized markdown files. It allows Hermes to extract runnable code from documentation and automatically sync edits back to the primary document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus facilitates cross-instance shared memory, allowing Hermes to remember past work and train replacement models. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data and fine-tuning models based on agent activity.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely integrates commerce rails, allowing for skill transactions using both USD and the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide Hermes with standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation models. They support precise technical requirements like hex color specification and structured JSON prompting.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both documentation and Python scaffolds on the fly, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities during use.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that coordinates multi-step missions using milestones and validation gates. It persists agent state in a disciplined on-disk model and generates handoff briefs to bridge separate sessions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It provides Hermes with real-time progress tracking and rollback capabilities to ensure safe task completion.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token usage during tool discovery. Its socket-based daemon supports hot-reloading, maintaining persistent connections between Hermes and various MCP servers.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It ensures high reliability by providing verifiable audit trails with file-and-line level provenance for every generated skill.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with integrated security scanning and threat detection. It features an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust and reliability scores for shared skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project provides a C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with full MCP integration. It allows Hermes to leverage .NET resources through progressive disclosure and standardized YAML frontmatter.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill grants Hermes the ability to manage files, calendars, and contacts within a Nextcloud environment via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication through HTTPS and includes a guided setup script for credential validation.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive collection of Nix packages for AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring they are always up-to-date through automated daily builds. It allows developers to execute agents instantly via Nix flakes, offering a unified and reproducible environment for CLI-based AI tools.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by implementing hardware-level sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that strictly enforces network and filesystem policies, making it the primary choice for running Hermes in sensitive or untrusted environments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository provides a dedicated NixOS module for the declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It excels at secure secret handling via sops-nix integration, catering to users who require a reproducible, system-level deployment on Nix-based infrastructure.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install solution provides a GUI-driven desktop agent equipped with 100 built-in tools and local model support. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex installations, featuring a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for local automation pipelines.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent for a ready-to-use sandbox. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using simple build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances and workspaces from a single web interface. It introduces advanced features like inter-agent delegation, automated task scheduling, and native integrations for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for headless operation and autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent's CLI directly through a browser. It is designed for easy management via Portainer and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds and deployment.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration platform that manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It integrates the Aegis review system to provide human-in-the-loop oversight and spend monitoring for complex task dispatching.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with visual organizational charts and durable memory across 23+ providers, including Hermes and Ollama. It distinguishes itself by allowing agents to learn new skills directly from conversation history while managing delegation within the swarm.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A specialized meta-optimization tool that uses an automated outer-loop search to evolve and improve harness code for Hermes Agent. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where two competing AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, battle for GitHub stars. The platform features a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution and scoreboard metrics as nine autonomous agents execute code and learning cycles.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A comprehensive pipeline that coordinates 17 specialized agents through a master orchestrator to handle research, implementation, and infrastructure tasks. It enforces strict quality control by requiring mandatory sign-offs from dedicated reviewer and tester agents at every stage.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: An AI-native software development lifecycle (SDLC) framework that provides Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It automates an eight-phase workflow, using graph-diff gates to ensure architectural integrity from initial planning to final health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: A delegation-focused framework that routes tasks between Hermes, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution through isolated workflows. It prioritizes operational safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits during agent handoffs.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based CLI tool provides a high-performance TUI for tracking token consumption and costs across multiple AI agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It features unique 3D contribution graph visualizations to help developers monitor their usage patterns across various model providers.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based configuration files for ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes. It simplifies UI customization by allowing new themes to inherit from default settings while only defining specific visual overrides.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth requirements. It specifically targets MLOps workflows by including built-in tools for vLLM inference, GGUF management, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Designed for autonomous agent control via MCP, this AI-native browser provides a visible interface for human supervision and action rollback. It enhances agent reliability by inferring structured schemas for web elements and maintaining durable, named sessions for complex tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md introduces a scoped framework for Hermes, allowing developers to define project-specific memories, tools, and instructions within individual folders. It enables seamless switching between global settings and local configurations, ensuring agents remain context-aware within specific workspaces.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter ensures the structural integrity of agent configurations and system prompts by detecting seven distinct failure modes without making LLM calls. It provides deterministic validation for YAML and JSON files, making it an essential tool for cost-free CI/CD integration and HERM v1.1 scoring.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations and workspace files to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of agent personas, memories, and local credentials to ensure continuity across platforms.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script automates the creation of a full Hermes Agent stack using Docker, LiteLLM, and Ollama for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, offering a zero-cost entry point for running agents with free model routing.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI in a mobile-friendly web format. It features a three-panel layout that allows users to browse workspace files and view inline previews alongside their chat sessions.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity, offering unified configuration for eight channels including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It includes robust session management, scheduled job tracking, and detailed token analytics for precise cost estimation.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace introduces 'Swarm Mode' for role-based dispatching of multiple persistent agents within a single interface. It provides a unified environment for chat, terminal access, and memory browsing while maintaining zero-fork compatibility with vanilla Hermes installations.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: Acting as a multi-agent gateway, this self-hosted dashboard allows users to start, stop, and configure individual agent profiles from a central hub. It features an integrated xterm.js terminal with full PTY support and granular system metrics for monitoring the agent stack.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that utilizes direct SSH connections to treat remote hosts as the single source of truth for agent data. The interface organizes the Hermes experience into specialized views for Kanban boards, cron jobs, and profile-aware session handling.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This React-based mobile dashboard brings the Hermes experience into the Telegram ecosystem as a Mini App with a 10-page UI. It supports background spawning for up to five concurrent agent instances and includes real-time system monitoring gauges for CPU and memory usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install portable GUI requires no administrative rights and comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for TTS, music, and ComfyUI. It features a unique dynamic tool maker that allows users to generate custom API wrappers at runtime.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process with a guided installer for local dependency resolution and environment setup. It provides a centralized GUI for managing 16 messaging gateways and 14 distinct toolsets with integrated full-text search for session history.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed for tracking agent sessions and associated costs. It specializes in transparency, offering FTS5 search capabilities for conversation history and read-only views for configuration and scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This chat-first workspace emphasizes extensibility through an integrated marketplace for discovering and installing new skills and MCP servers. It provides profile-scoped management of global memory and visualizes tool execution via SSE streaming timelines.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a glassmorphic design, this React 18 application includes a Kanban-style task board to track the progress of active tools and subagents. It combines a lightweight Python proxy with an integrated file browser and terminal for workspace-aware project management.
